name: Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit
name_original: Galende – vuelta por el Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Sierras Segundera y de Porto
description: Cycle the challenging 31.5-mile Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit, featuring glacial landscapes and stunning lake views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Galende, located within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, offers diverse terrain shaped by glacial landscapes. The region features the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula, surrounded by lush forests and the Tera River. Cyclists encounter varying gradients, from routes winding around the lake on mostly paved surfaces to challenging climbs in the Segundera and Porto mountain ranges. The area's natural features include high peaks, numerous lagoons, and deep canyons, providing a varied environment for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Galende The most popular road cycling route is Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit, a 31.5 miles (50.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route winds around the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda, a moderate 25.0 miles (40.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Tera River and Sanabria Lake, passing by historical sites. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto El Peñón loop from El Puente de Sanabria, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Galende is defined by a vast glacial lake, mountainous terrain, and the Tera River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Galende are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Galende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Galende?
A: Road cycling around Galende offers a diverse landscape shaped by glacial activity. You'll find routes winding around the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanabria Lake&lt;/a&gt; on mostly paved surfaces, alongside more challenging climbs in the Segundera and Porto mountain ranges. The area features high peaks, numerous lagoons, and deep canyons, providing varied gradients from moderate to significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels in Galende?
A: Yes, Galende offers a range of road cycling routes to suit various fitness levels. While there are no routes classified as 'easy', you'll find 6 moderate routes and 4 difficult routes. Moderate options, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda&lt;/a&gt;, offer enjoyable rides with manageable elevation, while routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023195029/laguna-de-los-peces-and-lake-sanabria-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit&lt;/a&gt; present a more demanding challenge.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along road cycling routes in Galende?
A: Many road cycling routes in Galende pass by stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites like the San Martín de Castañeda Monastery and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; with its castle are also often incorporated into rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Galende?
A: Yes, several popular road cycling routes around Galende are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023195029/laguna-de-los-peces-and-lake-sanabria-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda&lt;/a&gt;. These loops are great for exploring the region's diverse scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Galende?
A: The road cycling experience in Galende is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the region's varied terrain, from tranquil lake shores to challenging mountain passes, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in the unspoiled natural beauty of the Sanabria Lake Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Galende?
A: The region around Galende is generally best for road cycling during the warmer months, typically from late spring to early autumn. This period offers more favorable weather conditions for exploring the glacial landscapes, the shores of Sanabria Lake, and the mountainous areas without the challenges of winter snow or extreme heat.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation suitable for cyclists in Galende and nearby villages?
A: Yes, Galende and the surrounding charming villages, such as Puebla de Sanabria, offer various amenities. You'll find traditional architecture and local establishments where you can refuel with food and drinks. Many of these towns are accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts and provide accommodation options that cater to visitors exploring the Sanabria Lake Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Tera River?
A: Absolutely. The Tera River flows through Galende and is a prominent feature of the landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Monasterio de San Martín de Castañeda&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight this beautiful river, allowing cyclists to enjoy its scenic banks as part of their ride.

Q: How long are the road cycling routes typically in Galende?
A: The road cycling routes around Galende vary in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023195029/laguna-de-los-peces-and-lake-sanabria-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is about 31.5 miles (50.7 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Lago de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; extends to nearly 50 miles (79.5 km). You can find routes ranging from around 20 miles up to longer distances, offering options for different endurance levels.

Q: What kind of historical sites can I visit while road cycling in the Galende area?
A: The Galende area is rich in history. You can incorporate visits to sites like the San Martín de Castañeda Monastery, which offers a glimpse into the region's religious past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, with its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/910086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, is a notable point of interest often included in cycling routes, providing historical context and picturesque views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation challenges?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a challenge, Galende's mountainous terrain provides routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto El Peñón loop from El Puente de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, taking you through rugged landscapes and offering a rewarding workout.
tips: A small town in the municipality of Galende, Zamora near the border with Ourense, Galicia. Ribadelago, located near the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, has a tragic past. In 1959, the town was devastated by eight million cubic meters of water from the Vega de Tera reservoir.

The Sanabria Lake Natural Park, located in the municipality of Galende, collecting the waters of the Tera River. Beautiful place for adventure sports, there are many cycling routes around it through the Natural Park of Lake Sanabria and Sierras Segundera and Porto.

Subida Laguna de los Pèces is a great cycling climb. With a distance of 17.02 km and an elevation of 960 m, it's not an easy climb, although the average gradient is 4.2% going up to 1,724 m, the weather at the top can be cold so be prepared.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit?
faqs.answer: This road cycling circuit is considered difficult. The climb to Laguna de los Peces is particularly challenging, featuring an irregular gradient with ramps up to 9%. The exposed nature of some sections can also subject you to strong winds, adding to the difficulty.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling circuit?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. The road to Laguna de los Peces (ZA-103) is primarily paved, but be aware that the asphalt can be bumpy, cracked, or uneven due to temperature fluctuations. Around Lake Sanabria, while mostly paved, some longer circuits may include unpaved or difficult-to-ride segments, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular glacial landscapes. The route offers breathtaking vistas of the glacier-carved terrain and the massive Lake Sanabria, the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula. You'll pass through diverse habitats, including oak forests, and see various trees like alders, ashes, and willows. Numerous viewpoints provide panoramic views of the lake and its beaches.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks on the circuit?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Key highlights include the climb to Laguna de los Peces itself, offering stunning views as you ascend. You'll also cycle around Lake Sanabria, passing through charming villages like Ribadelago. The Neveira viewpoint is particularly recommended for its panoramic views, and the village of San Martín de Castañeda features a historic monastery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally from late spring to early autumn. During these months, the weather is more stable, and the risk of extreme temperatures or snow at higher elevations is lower. Be mindful that the exposed sections can be subject to strong winds at any time.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit?
faqs.answer: Parking is available at various points around Lake Sanabria, including near villages like Ribadelago. For the climb to Laguna de los Peces, there's a paved parking lot at 1,725 meters elevation, where the paved road ends.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de los Peces and Lake Sanabria Circuit dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sanabria Lake Natural Park generally welcomes visitors, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas, and be prepared for any unpaved sections where cycling might not be suitable for all dogs or require them to be carried.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Sanabria Lake Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sanabria Lake Natural Park for general cycling activities. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning protected areas, waste disposal, and wildlife. Some specific activities or events might require prior authorization.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the [CIMA ZM03] Laguna de los Peces ~ ZA-103 and [CIMA ZM03] Laguna de los Peces ~ Atajo Castañeda. You may also encounter sections of the Senda de los Monjes and the Senda de la Cueva de San Martín y Cañón del Tera.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages surrounding Lake Sanabria, such as Ribadelago, offer various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. These can be good spots to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding bike choice for this circuit?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, a robust road bike is recommended. Be prepared for potentially bumpy or cracked asphalt on the ZA-103 and the possibility of encountering unpaved sections around Lake Sanabria. If you plan to explore beyond the paved parking lot at Laguna de los Peces, you'll encounter a gravel road unsuitable for road bikes.

waypoints.name: Climb to Laguna de los Peces
